rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN GARDNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Ordinary And Extraordinary Compensation, And Reimbursement For Costs Advanced
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/17/2025 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed by the petitioner: 1) The total amount requested for each category of extraordinary service performed by the attorneys must also be alleged, in addition to the total hours spent on each category and applicable hourly rates. See CRC 7.702(4). 2) Allegations must be made in support of the Court's allowance of the attorneys' compensation for extraordinary services before final distribution under Probate Code section 10832. 3) A detailed description of ordinary legal services remaining to be performed must be provided. See CRC 7.701(3). In any event, the last 25 % of the attorneys' statutory compensation for ordinary services generally will not be allowed before final distribution. See LRSF 14.55(A). 4) A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (and not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 5) The address of the attorney for Mark Reiff isted on the proof of service does not appear current. Copies of all supplemental papers must also be served. See CRC 7.53(b) & section 1252(a). The following issues must be addressed by the objector: 1) The objection must be verified. See section 1021(a)(2); see also LRSF 14.15(C)(1) re omitted verification. 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (and not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN MCKEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 09/30/2024): 1) Items 2b, 2d, 3e(3), 3g(2)(a), & 3h(4) of the petition: i) If the petitioner is not a US resident, he is ineligible for appointment as administrator. See Probate Code section 8402(a)(4). ii) If the petitioner or any other heir who is not a US resident intends to nominate a proposed administrator under section 8465(a)(2): A) The nominee must be a California resident, as required by section 8465(d).B) Each factor of section 8465(d)(1)-(4) must be addressed. C) Good cause required by section 8465(e) must be shown for the Court to waive the nominee's bond (mere bond waivers signed by all heirs do not constitute good cause under that subdivision). D) The nominee's consent to serve required by LRSF 14.24 must filed with DE-147 and DE-147S forms. 2) Item 3a(2): Please provide a copy of an account statement, correspondence, or other documentation received from Chase Bank re the decedent's estate. 3) Item 3d: The estimated value of the estate is less than the small estate limit. Please briefly explain why the petitioner is opening this probate. 4) Item 8: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. ii) If Brian Kelly and Margaret MacLennan survived the decedent, CRC 7.51(e)(1) applies. The name and address of their personal representative must be provided, together with a copy of letters issued to the representative. Notice must also be given to the representative. If there is no personal representative: A) Did they die intestate? If so, the names and addresses of all heirs (including their surviving spouse or registered domestic partner if applicable) under intestate succession laws of their domicile must be provided. Notice must also be given to each heir. B) Did they die testate? If so, the names and addresses of all devisees named in their will must be provided. Notice must also be given to each devisee. 5) Sufficient information must be provided as to the application of intestate succession laws of Scotland to identify all heirs of the decedent. See Civil Code section 946; Estate of Grace (1948) 88 Cal. App. 2d 956, 959 ("the rules of inheritance as to personal property are to be determined by the laws of the state where the decedent was domiciled at the time of death"). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Proposed order must be prepared to mirror all relief that the petitioner is seeking. Petitioner should be aware that the ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO SEAL COURT RECORD entered 1/30/2025 applies to the court records only. It is not clear whether the current proposed order meets the County clerk's instruction stating, "if they wish to retain confidentiality, a Court Order Delayed Certificate of marriage (VS 122) is required that must stipulate that the marriage record be held confidential." rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF SEAN P HARRINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/2025 (Tues). The following issues must be cleared before 3/10/2025. Procedural Issues: (1) Declaration in Support of Petition to Establish Fact, Time, and Place of Birth [Judicial Council Form BMD-001A] must be filed as it is a mandatory use. (2) Petition #6 indicates that 3 pages are attached to the petition; however, there is only one-page attachment (certificate of marriage from the church) (3) Need more information to support the marriage. For example, when(time)/where(venue) the ceremony took place, photos, who performed the ceremony, a written declaration from the person who performed the ceremony, witnesses' statements describing the marriage or ceremony, and so on. (4) Please note that the proposed order does not use full names, if the Office of Vital Records requires consistency in the names, the proposed order may need to be revised.
